steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is A, $7yz^{3}+5yz$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Identify the like terms
steps.content: The terms $4yz^3$ and $3yz^3$ are like terms because they have the exact same variable part, $yz^3$. The term $5yz$ is not like them because it has $z$, not $z^3$.

steps.title: Combine coefficients only
steps.content:
Add the coefficients of the like terms and keep the variable part unchanged:
$$4yz^3+3yz^3=7yz^3$$
So the full expression becomes:
$$4yz^3+5yz+3yz^3=7yz^3+5yz$$

steps.title: Avoid the exponent trap
steps.content: Only coefficients of like terms are added. The exponent stays $3$ because $4yz^3$ and $3yz^3$ are being added, not multiplied.
